summary: Refactored fighter-related headers to clarify address notation and improve naming consistency around fighter variable and attribute structs. The PR body states that header addresses are virtual addresses with the `80` prefix omitted, renames `EntityVars`/`ev` to `FighterVars`/`fv`, and renames `SpecialAttrs` types located at `0x2D4` of `Fighter` to `DatAttrs`. No changed-file list, diff excerpt, or review comments were available in the slice, so conclusions are limited to the PR text.
what_changed: Documented or standardized that addresses appearing in fighter headers are virtual addresses with the leading `80` prefix omitted.
Renamed the associated short variable/member abbreviation from `ev` to `fv`.
Renamed types previously called `SpecialAttrs` at offset `0x2D4` of `Fighter` to `DatAttrs`.
smart_moves: Chose names that better scope the data to fighters: `FighterVars` is more explicit than the generic `EntityVars`.
Avoided overloading `SpecialAttrs` for data at `Fighter + 0x2D4`, using `DatAttrs` to distinguish data-table or DAT-backed attributes from special-move-specific attributes.
Included a reproducible Vim sorting command for maintaining address-ordered header annotations: `'<,'>!sort -t\  -k1n | uniq`.
decomp_lessons: When headers contain address annotations, make the address convention explicit; in this PR, fighter header addresses are virtual addresses with the `80` prefix omitted.
Prefer subsystem-specific struct names when a generic name obscures ownership or layout purpose.
Keep short aliases aligned with renamed types, as with `EntityVars`/`ev` becoming `FighterVars`/`fv`.
Differentiate attribute struct names by source or layout role; avoid calling all fighter attribute blocks `SpecialAttrs` if the data is better represented as `DatAttrs`.
For large header refactors, maintaining sorted and deduplicated address annotations can reduce noise and make future lookup easier.

naming_conventions.reason: More specifically names fighter-owned variable storage rather than generic entity variables.
For types located at `0x2D4` of `Fighter`, the PR text indicates `DatAttrs` is the preferred name over `SpecialAttrs`.

assembly_or_matching_tactics: No assembly matching changes were evidenced in the provided slice.
Header address annotations can be sorted and deduplicated with Vim using `'<,'>!sort -t\  -k1n | uniq`, which is useful for keeping decomp reference comments consistent.
follow_up_queries: Find usages of `FighterVars` to see the resulting fighter variable layout after PR #787.
Search for `DatAttrs` definitions around `Fighter` offset `0x2D4` to understand the attribute block renamed in this PR.
Check later PRs touching fighter headers for continued use of omitted-`80` virtual address annotations.
